related.description: Our client’s home was architecturally designed to follow in a curve the wonderful views of a pond, meadow, and extensive garden. Though she had lived in the house for many years while raising her family, she felt the interior no longer reflected her sensibilities. At our first meeting, she specifically asked us to bring femininity and softness into her living space focusing on the master bedroom, living room, and family room.

related.description: Color leads the way to the dramatic living room and  bedrooms on the stair risers, pulling tints of coastal color from every  room in the house. Vibrant cut paper collages created by Karen Beckwith  celebrate the swim and neutral beach pebbles stacked in a cairn. Hand  painted watercolor paper forms the negative space around a cut paper  surf board in the foyer, hinting at the watery views down the hall.

related.description: The family came to us wanting a home that could hold everyone- kids, cousins, friends - and capture the spirit of summer by the lake. They needed spaces that worked hard: mudrooms for gear, bunk rooms for overflow, and open gathering areas for long weekends together. The result is a house that feels effortless and alive; layered with natural textures, and filled with light, laughter, and the easy rhythm of lake life.
related.title: Summer Hill House

related.description: When does modern become truly modern? This Berkshire retreat began its modern life in the 70s and was partially renovated in the 90s, leaving it with a bit of an identity crisis of worn versus nicely improved. Our clients commissioned us to design a renovation that leaves the home with a richer palette of materials and a clean point of view- with artistic moments in each of the spaces. A self-assured personality has evolved.

title: The Crafted Home

related.bottomLeftText:
We have relationships with talented makers, builders, architects, & installers- whom we trust to help us execute the vision. Beckwith Barrow will manage every detail from concept to completion so you can enjoy the transformation without the stress.
We bring deep creativity, transparency, and care to everything we do. We sweat the details so you don’t have to, managing your project as if it were our own home.
related.title: Who We Are
related.topRightText:
What began over 25 years ago as a solo practice (Karen Beckwith Creative) has grown into a true family collaboration. As a mother–daughter team, Beckwith Barrow reflects our shared belief that the best homes tell the stories of the people who live in them.
Our job is to help you realize that home- one that feels unmistakably you, elevated beyond what you could have imagined on your own.
Design, to us, is both art and problem solving- the creative grounded in function and executed with precision.

related.description: This contemporary farmhouse honors the client's personal history in the area and embraces the sweeping landscape. This was a labor of love for all the collaborators involved. Architectural design by Burr and McCallum Architects. Interior design by Beckwith Barrow. Home construction by Eric Zahn Builders. Kitchen, bath and cabinetry by Keeler Concepts by Design.

related.description: Our clients purchased a 1980s modern Rhode Island beach cottage with long distance views to the sea from the second floor living space. The footprint of the house is small but they requested a living room that could accommodate up to 8 guests seated. A triangularly shaped second floor deck overlooks land and sea. Our objective was to blur the division of inside and outside: bringing the blue & turquoise palette into the home. The tall glass wall separating the deck from the family room required us to decorate as if it was all one room.
related.title: Coastal Hideaway
